entities.summary: Enumeration of all valid lifecycle states for a generation request

detail.name: SequenceStatus
detail.description: WAITING (queued), RUNNING (active on GPU), FINISHED_STOPPED, FINISHED_LENGTH, FINISHED_ABORT.
detail.why: Explicit states prevent invalid operations — cannot free blocks of a running sequence

teaches.explain: Use an enum to restrict valid states. Methods check state before acting. Prevents impossible combinations at compile time.

entities.summary: Tracks generation state: tokens, sampling params, logical-physical block mapping

detail.name: Sequence
detail.description: Full lifecycle of one generation request: from prompt tokens through generation to finish.
detail.why: Each request has unique state (tokens generated, KV blocks used) that must persist across scheduling steps

teaches.explain: Object transitions between well-defined states (WAITING→RUNNING→FINISHED). Only certain transitions allowed.
Indirection between logical address and physical location. Enables relocation without invalidating references.

detail.design: Separates token management from block management within same object for locality
detail.convention: Sequence is read-only outside engine internals

entities.summary: Initialize sequence with prompt tokens and sampling configuration

detail.name: __init__
detail.description: Creates new sequence with initial prompt tokens, assigns unique ID, allocates first logical block.
detail.why: Every generation starts here — establishes the baseline state before scheduling can begin

teaches.explain: Constructor sets up minimal valid state. Object is usable immediately after __init__ without extra setup calls.

entities.summary: Return total number of tokens (prompt + generated)

detail.name: __len__
detail.description: Enables len(sequence) syntax. Returns prompt length + completion length.

teaches.explain: Implement __len__ to make class work with len(). Part of Python sized protocol. Enables intuitive API for collection-like objects.

detail.name: __getitem__
detail.description: Enables sequence[i] syntax for token access.

teaches.explain: Implement __getitem__ for indexing syntax obj[i]. Part of sequence protocol. Can also enable iteration and slicing.

entities.summary: Predicate checking if generation has terminated

detail.name: is_finished
detail.description: Returns True if status is any FINISHED_ variant. Used by scheduler to know when to free resources.

teaches.explain: Method returning bool that encapsulates a business-logic check. Centralizes condition rather than repeating enum checks.

entities.summary: Property returning count of generated tokens so far

detail.name: num_completion_tokens
detail.description: Computed property: total length minus prompt length.

teaches.explain: @property makes method accessible as attribute. Computed on access, not stored. Keeps derived values always consistent with source.
List slicing in Python creates a copy. For large sequences, consider memoryview or numpy views to avoid copying.
list[start:end] extracts sub-sequence. Omit start for 0, omit end for len. Negative indices count from end.

entities.summary: Compute number of KV cache blocks used by this sequence

detail.name: num_blocks
detail.description: Ceiling division of total tokens by block size.

teaches.explain: -(-a // b) or math.ceil(a/b). Computes how many fixed-size blocks needed to hold variable-size content.

entities.summary: Count tokens in the last (potentially partial) block

detail.name: last_block_num_tokens
detail.description: total_tokens % block_size, or block_size if exactly full.

teaches.explain: n % block_size gives fill level of last block. If 0, block is full (use block_size). Common in paged/chunked systems.

entities.summary: Get logical block index for a given token position

detail.name: block
detail.description: Maps token position to its containing block: position // block_size.

teaches.explain: block_index = position // block_size. Fundamental operation in any paged/blocked memory system. O(1) mapping.

entities.summary: Extends sequence by one generated token, managing block boundaries

detail.name: append_token
detail.description: Appends new token to completion, allocates new logical block if current is full.
detail.why: Token-by-token autoregressive generation — each step adds exactly one token

teaches.explain: Model generates one token per step, using all previous tokens as context. Append loop continues until stop condition.

detail.perf: Called once per token per sequence per step — must be O(1)

entities.summary: Custom pickle serialization excluding non-serializable fields

detail.name: __getstate__
detail.description: Controls what gets serialized when sequence is pickled (for preemption/migration).

teaches.explain: __getstate__ controls serialization. Return dict of what to save. Exclude file handles, locks, GPU tensors that cant be pickled.

entities.summary: Custom pickle deserialization restoring sequence state

detail.name: __setstate__
detail.description: Restores sequence from pickled state, re-initializes transient fields.

teaches.explain: Counterpart to __getstate__. Receives saved dict, must reconstruct full object. Re-initialize transient resources (connections, caches).
